2606.27539.title: Benchmarking Multi-Modal Graph-based Social Media Popularity Prediction
2606.27539.paper_summary: Social media popularity prediction aims to forecast the future reach or influence of online content from early-stage observations. Accurate prediction enables key downstream applications, such as advertising optimization and strategic content planning by users, creators, and platforms. Despite substantial progress, existing popularity prediction works often fail to jointly consider multimodal content and temporal social interaction signals. Moreover, the literature remains highly fragmented across datasets, modalities, observation windows, prediction targets, and evaluation protocols. This fragmentation prevents fair comparison and obscures a systematic understanding of how textual, visual, temporal, and interaction-based signals jointly shape popularity dynamics. To address these challenges, we introduce MMG-Pop, a Multi-modal Graph-based Popularity Prediction benchmark, which unifies datasets, modalities, temporal interaction signals, and representative baselines under a standardized evaluation protocol. Furthermore, we propose MMG-PopNet, a unified multi-modal graph-based network that jointly models the aforementioned multi-modal signals and graph-structured social interactions. Extensive experiments on MMG-Pop, comprising four datasets across Bluesky and Reddit platforms, demonstrate the superior performance of MMG-PopNet and yield new insights into cross-platform training generalization, multi-task prediction benefits, multi-modality contributions, and LLM prediction limitation. These findings establish a unified foundation for future research on social dynamics modeling and intervention under heterogeneous modalities and socially-aware agentic ecosystem paradigms.
2606.27539.paper_summary_zh: 社交媒體人氣預測旨在從早期觀察中預測在線內容的未來觸及或影響力。準確的預測使關鍵的下游應用成為可能，例如用戶、創作者和平台的廣告優化和戰略內容規劃。儘管取得了重大進展，但現有的人氣預測工作往往未能共同考慮多模態內容和時間社交互動信號。此外，文獻在數據集、模態、觀察窗口、預測目標和評估協議方面仍然高度碎片化。這種碎片化阻礙了公平比較，並模糊了對文本、視覺、時間和基於互動的信號如何共同塑造人氣動態的系統理解。為了解決這些挑戰，我們引入了MMG-Pop，一個基於多模態圖的人氣預測基準，該基準在標準化評估協議下統一了數據集、模態、時間互動信號和代表性基準。此外，我們提出了MMG-PopNet，一個統一的基於多模態圖的網絡，該網絡共同建模上述多模態信號和圖結構社交互動。對MMG-Pop的廣泛實驗，包括來自Bluesky和Reddit平台的四個數據集，展示了MMG-PopNet的卓越性能，並對跨平台訓練的泛化、多任務預測的好處、多模態貢獻和LLM預測的限制提供了新的見解。這些發現為未來在異質模態和社會意識代理生態系統範式下的社會動態建模和干預研究奠定了統一的基礎。
2606.27539.author: Utkarsh Sahu et.al.
2606.27539.authors: Utkarsh Sahu, Zhisheng Qi, Li Zhu, Yizhao Yang, Jun Li, Ryan Rossi, Yu Wang
